# Asia Tech Visitor & Meeting Copilot

## Business Scenario

Yeeflow will attend Asia Tech x Singapore 2026 from May 20 to May 22, 2026. The event team needs a lightweight Yeeflow application to capture booth visitors, scheduled meetings, ad hoc discussions, name-card or badge images, AI extraction results, lead-fit analysis, follow-up tasks, and email drafts.

The application is a new `.yap` app package named `Asia Tech Visitor & Meeting Copilot`. It is designed for import into `https://<yourdomain>.yeeflow.com/` as a sandbox/runtime-test app first.

## V1 Scope

V1 includes:

- Contacts, Companies, Follow-up Tasks, and Event Meetings data lists.
- Event Command Center dashboard.
- AI Capture Workspace dashboard.
- Event Booth Assistant Copilot with quick prompts.
- Name Card & Badge Extraction Agent.
- Lead Fit & Follow-up Advisor Agent.
- Contacts new-item workflow that calls the Lead Fit Agent, calls an Outreach Email Generator Agent, and conditionally sends follow-up email when a valid recipient email is produced.
- Image/file field on Contacts for name-card or badge capture.
- App-resource access tool structures scoped only to the generated local app resources.

V1 does not include:

- real visitor data, attendee data, or name-card images
- fixed real-recipient email configuration
- external API, Outlook, SharePoint, HTTP, OAuth, or CRM connectors
- production credentials or secrets
- approval forms or approval workflows
- custom code dashboards
- document library resources, unless a later focused pass proves a stronger reason to add them

## Business Assumptions

- Users will capture records manually or through Copilot-assisted flows during the event.
- Follow-up email content is generated by AI. The workflow can send only through a `MailTask` whose recipient, subject, and body come from workflow variables produced by the Outreach Email Generator Agent.
- AI classification is advisory; the team reviews lead type, score, and email draft before acting.
- Contact image extraction, lead analysis, outreach email generation, and Send Email execution are runtime-sensitive because they can call live AI, mutate records, and send email.
- Runtime testing should first verify import/open/configuration only, then optionally create a disposable fake contact if safe.

## Data Model

### Contacts

Purpose: Store people met at the booth or during event meetings.

Fields:

- Full Name
- Company
- Job Title
- Email
- Phone
- LinkedIn / Website
- Country / Region
- Source Type: Booth Visitor, Scheduled Meeting, Walk-in, Referral, Partner Intro
- Event Day
- Booth / Meeting Location
- Name Card / Badge Image
- User Notes / Chat Comments
- Extracted Raw Text
- AI Summary
- Lead Type: Customer, Partner, Investor, Vendor, Other
- Fit Score
- Fit Reason
- Suggested Value Proposition
- Suggested Next Step
- Follow-up Email Subject
- Follow-up Email Body
- Follow-up Status: New, Needs Review, Ready to Send, Sent, Not Relevant
- Owner
- Created From: Manual, Copilot, Image Extraction, Import
- Extraction Status: Pending, Extracted, Needs Review, Failed
- Workflow Status

Views:

- All Contacts
- Today’s Contacts
- Needs Review
- High Fit Leads
- Partner Opportunities
- Ready to Follow Up
- Missing Email

### Companies

Purpose: Store organization-level context.

Fields:

- Company Name
- Website
- Industry
- Company Size
- Country / Region
- Company Type
- Interest Area
- Potential Relationship: Customer, Partner, Both, Unknown
- Yeeflow Fit Summary
- Key Pain Points
- Suggested Use Cases
- Notes

Views:

- All Companies
- Potential Customers
- Potential Partners
- High Fit Companies

### Follow-up Tasks

Purpose: Track next actions after a contact is captured.

Fields:

- Task Title
- Related Contact
- Related Company
- Task Type: Send Email, Schedule Meeting, Share Demo, Partner Discussion, Add to CRM, Other
- Due Date
- Priority
- Status: Open, In Progress, Done, Cancelled
- Owner
- Suggested By AI
- Task Notes

Views:

- My Open Tasks
- Due Today
- High Priority
- AI Suggested Tasks
- Completed Tasks

### Event Meetings

Purpose: Track scheduled or ad hoc meetings during the event.

Fields:

- Meeting Title
- Contact
- Company
- Meeting Date/Time
- Meeting Location
- Meeting Type
- Discussion Notes
- Outcome
- Follow-up Required
- Related Follow-up Task

## Copilot

### Event Booth Assistant

The Copilot is app-contained and uses only local application resources or app-contained Agent references. Copilot chat does not send email directly; the Contacts workflow owns follow-up email delivery after AI email validation.

Quick prompts:

- Add this visitor from a name card photo
- Search contacts from today
- Show high-fit partner leads
- Create follow-up tasks for today’s new contacts
- Draft a follow-up email for this contact
- Summarize all booth visitors from today

Runtime-safe first test:

- Confirm Copilot resource appears.
- Open configuration.
- Confirm quick prompts appear.
- Confirm local components reference generated local app resources only.
- Do not trigger live Copilot chat until safe scope is explicitly chosen.

## AI Agents

### Name Card & Badge Extraction Agent

Inputs:

- `badge_or_card_image` (`img`)
- `user_comments` (`text`)
- `event_day` (`text`)
- `owner_context` (`text`)

Outputs:

- `extraction_summary` (`text`)
- `needs_review` (`text`)

Expected behavior:

- Extract visible contact details from a badge or name card.
- Store raw text and user comments.
- Create or update local Contact and Company records through the app-resource access tool when safely executed.
- Mark uncertain records as Needs Review.

Generation status:

- Agent resource and tool structure are generated from export-proven app-bound resource patterns.
- Live image extraction execution is safety-deferred.

### Lead Fit & Follow-up Advisor Agent

Inputs:

- `contact_item_id` (`text`)
- `contact_payload` (`text`)
- `company_payload` (`text`)
- `notes` (`text`)

Outputs:

- `lead_type` (`text`)
- `fit_score` (`text`)
- `fit_reason` (`text`)
- `suggested_next_step` (`text`)
- `email_subject` (`text`)
- `email_body` (`text`)

Expected behavior:

- Classify lead type as Customer, Partner, Investor, Vendor, or Other.
- Score fit from 0 to 100.
- Draft follow-up tasks and email content.
- Update local Contact and Company records and create Follow-up Tasks through app-resource access tools when safely executed.

Generation status:

- Agent resource and Contacts workflow AI node are generated.
- Workflow execution remains safety-deferred until an isolated runtime test is approved.

### Asia Tech Outreach Email Generator

Inputs:

- `contact_item_id` (`text`)
- `contact_email` (`text`)
- `contact_payload` (`text`)
- `company_payload` (`text`)
- `notes` (`text`)
- `lead_fit` (`text`)
- `value_context` (`text`)

Outputs:

- `is_valid_email` (`text`, expected `Yes` or `No`)
- `recipient_email` (`text`)
- `email_subject` (`text`)
- `email_body` (`richtext`)
- `value_statement` (`text`)

Expected behavior:

- Validate whether the Contact email is usable for outreach.
- Identify the most relevant Yeeflow value for the contact and company.
- Generate a concise professional follow-up email that mentions Asia Tech x Singapore 2026 and includes a meeting/demo CTA.
- Return `No` and an empty recipient when the email is missing, malformed, or unsafe for outreach.

## Workflows

### Contacts New Item Workflow

Trigger:

- Contacts data list new item trigger via `FlowMappings[].Setting.NewTrigger = true`.

Workflow:

1. Start when a new Contact is added.
2. AI Assistant node calls the Lead Fit & Follow-up Advisor Agent.
3. AI Assistant node calls the Asia Tech Outreach Email Generator Agent.
4. The Outreach Agent maps `is_valid_email`, `recipient_email`, `email_subject`, `email_body`, and `value_statement` into workflow variables.
5. If `IsEmailValid = Yes`, the workflow sends the email with a `MailTask` using workflow-variable recipient, subject, and body.
6. If `IsEmailValid != Yes`, the workflow skips the Send Email node and ends.

Runtime boundary:

- Import/open/designer checks are safe.
- Do not run the workflow against real contacts during initial runtime testing.
- Only test Send Email with a disposable contact record and an email recipient that is explicitly safe to receive the test message.

### Deferred Workflows

- Event Meeting follow-up workflow is deferred to a later proof pass.
- End-of-day scheduled summary workflow is deferred because automated AI/email behavior would require a separate safe schedule and recipient setup.

## Dashboards

### Event Command Center

Includes:

- operational KPI cards
- recent Contacts collection
- open Follow-up Tasks collection
- lead/review guidance text

Generation note:

- KPI cards are generated as conservative static summary placeholders because data-bound dashboard summary widgets over this new schema require runtime proof. Collections are data-bound to local lists.

### AI Capture Workspace

Includes:

- Copilot usage guidance
- recent extracted contacts collection
- contacts missing email collection
- follow-up email review list

## Safety Boundaries

- No secrets, tokens, credentials, endpoints, OAuth metadata, or external connectors are generated.
- No real attendee, visitor, company, or business-card data is seeded.
- No real image binaries are embedded.
- Email content is saved as drafts only.
- The generated workflow is safe for designer visibility checks, not for automatic execution proof.
- Runtime proof must distinguish local validation, import/open proof, configuration proof, and execution proof.

## Validation Plan

Run:

- MJS syntax check.
- JSON parse check for generated app definition and reports.
- `validate-yap-package.js`.
- `validate-yap-graph.js`.
- `validate-ydl-list.js` for each generated data list.
- `scripts/inspect-yap-materialization.mjs`.
- `scripts/inspect-agent-copilot-resources.mjs`.
- wrapper round trip through `build-yap-wrapper.js` and package validation.
- uniqueness checks for `FieldID`, `ListID`, and `ListDataID`.
- ReplaceIds scope checks.
- secret/private-data safety scan.
- `git diff --check`.

## Runtime Test Plan

Target: `https://<yourdomain>.yeeflow.com/`

Safe-first checks:

- package imports
- app opens
- Contacts, Companies, Follow-up Tasks, and Event Meetings lists open
- dashboards open
- views render
- Contacts image upload field is visible in the form
- Copilot resource appears and configuration opens
- quick prompts appear
- AI Agents appear and configuration opens
- app-resource access tool configuration references local resources
- Contacts workflow appears
- workflow designer opens
- AI Assistant node opens and references Lead Fit Agent
- no real email is sent
- no external API call occurs

Optional controlled checks:

- Create one fake disposable contact manually.
- Do not upload a real badge or name card.
- Use a harmless dummy image only if execution scope is explicitly approved.
- Do not publish or run the workflow unless the sandbox side effects are explicitly accepted.

## Known Risks

- App-contained AI Agent and Copilot structures are export-proven, but generated import/runtime behavior needs focused confirmation.
- AI image extraction can call live AI and mutate records; execution proof must be separately approved and documented.
- Contacts data-list workflow trigger-condition settings still need a focused export/runtime study before the generator rule is considered fully proven.
- Data-bound KPI/chart dashboard controls over this generated schema are not claimed in v1.
- App-resource access tool permissions and runtime mutation scope remain runtime-sensitive.
